title: AURA
description: AI-powered smart home automation platform that integrates facial recognition, machine learning, and IoT technologies to create a personalized living environment.

detail.overview: AURA combines computer vision, edge computing, and automation to deliver a personalized smart home experience that learns resident preferences over time.
highlights: Face recognition unlocks personalized lighting and climate control for each household member.
Adaptive schedules anticipate user behavior and reduce energy consumption.
Secure edge processing keeps sensitive biometric data local to the device.

problem: Home automation systems struggled to personalize behavior across multiple occupants.
Users needed better security than password-based control panels for critical devices.
Existing smart home technology required complex setup and avoided offline-sensitive data handling.
solution: AURA uses face recognition to identify residents and apply custom living preferences automatically.
A modular IoT architecture connects sensors, cameras, and appliances through a lightweight edge controller.
The system balances convenience with security by keeping biometric inference on the Raspberry Pi gateway.

architecture.overview: AURA is built as a hybrid edge/cloud system that processes vision and device control locally while storing usage metadata in a lightweight database for analytics.
components: Raspberry Pi gateway running Python and OpenCV
FaceNet-based recognition service for resident identification
MQTT messaging bus for appliance and sensor orchestration
SQLite/MySQL persistence for schedules, profiles, and event logging
notes: The facial recognition pipeline was optimized to run efficiently on low-power hardware.
IoT devices communicate through MQTT with secure TLS transport.
Personalization rules are evaluated in real time and cached for reduced latency.

features: Real-time face recognition for contextual automation.
Adaptive climate control tied to occupant presence.
Multi-zone lighting scenes triggered by user identity.
Cross-device preferences synchronized through the local database.
challenges: Optimizing computer vision for Raspberry Pi hardware without sacrificing accuracy.
Designing a flexible rule engine to support varying resident behaviors.
Maintaining privacy while still enabling meaningful personalization.
learnings: Edge-first design improves privacy and responsiveness for home automation.
Simple UX patterns are essential for non-technical users managing complex systems.
A modular architecture made it easier to add new device integrations later.
futureEnhancements: Add natural language voice control for hands-free operation.
Support guest profiles with temporary access policies.
Integrate solar and battery energy forecasting for greener automation.

screenshots.caption: Central control dashboard with occupancy-aware automation.
Mobile access for lighting, climate, and security.

title: HyperTransit
description: A mobile-first transit platform built for live bus tracking, secure ticketing, and seamless commuter experience.

detail.overview: HyperTransit delivers instant transit visibility and digital ticketing for city commuters through a polished mobile interface and real-time transport updates.
highlights: Live vehicle tracking with map integration for rider confidence.
Digital pass creation and one-tap payments for seamless boarding.
Accessible route planning for urban commuters across multiple bus lines.

problem: Commuters lacked real-time visibility into bus arrival times and route progress.
Ticket purchase was dependent on physical kiosks or cash payments.
Transport operators needed a simpler way to manage passes and user access without manual reconciliation.
solution: Built a Flutter mobile app that synchronizes live vehicle locations with route schedules.
Integrated Firebase authentication and payments to support digital tickets and one-time passes.
Provided an intuitive rider workflow from route discovery to boarding confirmation.

architecture.overview: HyperTransit combines a Flutter frontend with Firebase backend services for realtime updates, authentication, and payment workflows.
components: Flutter mobile application for Android and iOS
Firebase Authentication for secure sign-in and user profiles
Cloud Firestore for route, schedule, and ticket state
Payment integration for one-time pass checkout
notes: Route state updates were pushed through Firestore listeners for immediate bus status refresh.
The app uses offline-first caching to retain route data during intermittent network coverage.
User profiles store ticket history and preferred routes for faster rebooking.

features: Live bus tracking with route overlays and ETA updates.
Digital ticket wallet with one-time and pass purchases.
Route search and saved trips for repeat commuters.
Secure login and user profile management via Firebase.
challenges: Synchronizing mobile UI state with real-time transit updates efficiently.
Balancing offline access with accurate ticket validation.
Designing a clean payment flow that minimized user friction.
learnings: Real-time transport data requires lightweight event updates to preserve mobile performance.
A consistent UX flow is critical for commuter trust when purchasing tickets on the go.
Firebase provided a strong foundation for rapid prototyping with authentication and realtime sync.
futureEnhancements: Add multimodal trip planning across bus, metro, and last-mile options.
Support scheduled ticket reminders and delay alerts.
Introduce driver-side validation tools for faster boarding.

screenshots.caption: Live bus location and estimated arrival times
Digital pass wallet for fast boarding
